attrs.alt: Entra External ID for Customers CIAM architecture

content.text: What is External ID for Customers?

Microsoft Entra External ID for Customers is Microsoft's Customer Identity and Access Management (CIAM) solution. It provides a dedicated tenant separate from your workforce Entra ID where end-customers register, sign in, and manage their profiles for your consumer-facing apps. The tenant issues standard OAuth2/OIDC tokens your apps validate the same way they would for workforce users.

External ID for Customers replaced Azure AD B2C as the preferred CIAM platform. It uses the same Microsoft Entra infrastructure and admin experience but is optimized for high-volume consumer sign-up flows and custom branding.
External ID vs Workforce Entra ID vs Azure AD B2C

A CIAM tenant is a separate Entra ID tenant with the 'External' configuration. Create one from the Azure portal by selecting 'External' when creating a new tenant. Register your app in this tenant — not in your workforce tenant.

# Create an External ID tenant for customers (CIAM)
# Requires: Azure subscription, Global Administrator role

# Via Azure portal: Azure Active Directory → Manage tenants → Create
# Select: External (for customers and business partners) → Customer scenario

# After creation, register your app in the External ID tenant
Connect-MgGraph -TenantId 'your-ciam-tenant.onmicrosoft.com' \
    -Scopes 'Application.ReadWrite.All'

# Register a web app with authorization code + PKCE flow
$appBody = @{
    displayName = 'My Customer App'
    signInAudience = 'AzureADandPersonalMicrosoftAccount'
    web = @{
        redirectUris = @('https://myapp.contoso.com/auth/callback')
        implicitGrantSettings = @{
            enableAccessTokenIssuance = $false
            enableIdTokenIssuance     = $false
        }
    }
    requiredResourceAccess = @()
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5

$app = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST \
    -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/applications' \
    -Body $appBody -ContentType 'application/json'
Write-Host "App registered: $($app.appId)"

User flows define the end-to-end sign-up and sign-in experience: which identity providers are offered, which attributes are collected, how the UI looks, and what validation hooks run. A single External ID tenant can host multiple user flows bound to different apps.

Apps acquire tokens from the External ID tenant using standard MSAL libraries or any OAuth2/OIDC client. The authority URL uses the .ciamlogin.com domain for customer tenants. MSAL handles PKCE, token caching, and silent renewal automatically.

# Python: acquire a token using MSAL for a CIAM tenant (public client / auth code)
# pip install msal
import msal

TENANT_ID   = 'your-ciam-tenant.onmicrosoft.com'
CLIENT_ID   = 'your-app-client-id'
AUTHORITY   = f'https://your-ciam-tenant.ciamlogin.com/{TENANT_ID}'
SCOPES      = ['openid', 'profile', 'offline_access']
REDIRECT_URI = 'http://localhost:5000/callback'

app = msal.PublicClientApplication(
    CLIENT_ID,
    authority=AUTHORITY,
)

# Initiate device code flow (suitable for CLI tools)
flow = app.initiate_device_flow(scopes=SCOPES)
print(flow['message'])  # Prints: 'Go to https://... and enter code XXXXX'

result = app.acquire_token_by_device_flow(flow)
if 'access_token' in result:
    print('Access token acquired')
    print(f"Token expires in: {result['expires_in']}s")
else:
    print(f"Error: {result.get('error_description')}")

API connectors let you plug custom logic into the sign-up flow — validate email domains, enrich attributes from a CRM, or block high-risk sign-ups before the account is created. The connector is an HTTPS endpoint (Azure Function or any webhook) that returns a Continue or ShowBlockPage response.

Extend the customer user object with your own attributes — loyalty tier, subscription plan, region preference. Custom attributes are stored as extension attributes on the user object and can be included in the sign-up form and in tokens.

# Add a custom attribute (extension attribute) to customer profiles
# Custom attributes appear in the sign-up form and are stored on the user object
$attrBody = @{
    displayName = 'LoyaltyTier'
    description = 'Customer loyalty tier: bronze, silver, gold, platinum'
    dataType    = 'string'
} | ConvertTo-Json

Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST \
    -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/userFlowAttributes' \
    -Body $attrBody -ContentType 'application/json'

# Retrieve a specific customer user and read custom attributes
$userId = "aaaabbbb-1234-5678-9abc-def012345678"
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users/$userId" +
         '?$select=id,displayName,mail,extension_<appId>_LoyaltyTier'
# Replace <appId> with the b2cExtensionsApp's appId (without hyphens)
Company branding: logo, background image, accent color, and custom HTML/CSS per flow.
Custom domain: map yourdomain.com/signin instead of exposing the tenant domain to end users.
Native authentication API: embed the auth UI directly in mobile/desktop apps without a browser redirect (avoids webview-in-app anti-patterns).
Languages: built-in localization for 36 languages with JSON overrides for custom strings.

Connect Google, Facebook, or Apple as sign-in options by registering an OAuth app in each provider's developer console and configuring the credentials in the External ID tenant. Social sign-ins do not require a password — customers sign in with their existing social account.

# Add Google as a social identity provider in the External ID tenant
# Prereq: Create an OAuth 2.0 app in Google Cloud Console
# Authorized redirect URI: https://<tenant>.ciamlogin.com/<tenant>/oauth2/authresp

$googleIdp = @{
    '@odata.type' = '#microsoft.graph.socialIdentityProvider'
    displayName   = 'Google'
    identityProviderType = 'Google'
    clientId     = '123456789-abcdefgh.apps.googleusercontent.com'
    clientSecret = $env:GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET
} | ConvertTo-Json

Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST \
    -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/identityProviders' \
    -Body $googleIdp -ContentType 'application/json'
Write-Host 'Google social IdP registered'

# After registering, add Google to the user flow's identity providers:
# GET the flow, then PATCH onAuthenticationMethodLoadStart to include Google's provider ID

Replace the default ciamlogin.com domain with your own branded domain (e.g., login.yourapp.com) so customers see your brand throughout the authentication experience. The custom domain is backed by Microsoft's CDN and requires only a CNAME and TXT DNS record.

# Configure a custom domain for your External ID tenant
# Customers will see login.yourapp.com instead of yourtenant.ciamlogin.com

# Step 1: Add a custom domain in the External ID tenant
# (Portal: External Identities → Custom URL domain)
# Add CNAME: login.yourapp.com → yourtenant.ciamlogin.com

# Step 2: Verify the domain
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST \
    -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/domains' \
    -Body (@{ id = 'login.yourapp.com' } | ConvertTo-Json) \
    -ContentType 'application/json'

# Step 3: Add a TXT record to prove ownership
$verify = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
    -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/domains/login.yourapp.com/verificationDnsRecords'
$verify.value | ForEach-Object {
    Write-Host "Add TXT record: $($_.text) for label: $($_.label)"
}

# Step 4: Trigger verification
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST \
    -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/domains/login.yourapp.com/verify'
Write-Host 'Custom domain verified. Update app redirect URIs to use login.yourapp.com'

Require MFA at the user flow level. External ID supports email OTP and TOTP authenticator apps. Unlike workforce MFA, customer MFA is opt-in or step-up — enforced contextually rather than always-on, to balance security and UX friction.

Include custom attributes and user profile fields in the tokens your app receives. A claims mapping policy maps user object attributes (including custom extension attributes) to JWT claim names your app expects.

When migrating customers from a legacy IdP, bulk-create accounts via Graph API using the emailAddress signInType. Set forceChangePasswordNextSignIn to require the customer to set a new password on first login, or use a just-in-time migration API connector approach.

The External ID tenant's Sign-in Logs and Audit Logs are accessible via Log Analytics just like a workforce tenant. Track sign-in success rates, new registrations, and identity provider distribution to understand usage patterns.

// Monitor customer sign-in activity in the External ID tenant
// Sign-in Logs in the CIAM tenant behave identically to workforce tenant
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| summarize
    TotalSignIns    = count(),
    UniqueUsers     = dcount(UserId),
    SuccessRate     = round(100.0 * countif(ResultType == 0) / count(), 1),
    TopIdP          = arg_max(count(), AuthenticationProtocol)
    by bin(TimeGenerated, 1d), AppDisplayName
| order by TimeGenerated desc

// Track sign-up events (new customer registrations)
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where OperationName == 'Create user'
| where InitiatedBy.app.displayName contains 'user flow'
| summarize NewRegistrations = count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 1d)
| order by TimeGenerated desc
External ID for Customers is a dedicated CIAM tenant separate from your workforce Entra ID, optimized for consumer-facing apps.
User flows define sign-up/sign-in experiences including IdPs, attribute collection, UI branding, and API connector hooks.
Apps authenticate using standard OIDC/OAuth2 with MSAL or any standards-compliant client; the authority is your-tenant.ciamlogin.com.
API connectors hook into the sign-up flow to run custom validation, enrichment, or blocking logic.
Custom attributes extend the user profile and can be collected during sign-up and emitted in tokens.
Pricing is MAU-based with 50,000 free MAU per month — suitable for early-stage products at zero cost.
